mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction starts and rising mortgage debt service capacity indicates robust housing demand, which historically leads equity markets higher over the next quarter as construction supply chains expand.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns in more than 50 percent of instances over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding the 100 index level signals supply-side resilience and operational efficiency, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are flat or negative when INDPRO remains above 100 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month consumer price inflation exceeding 0.4 percent forces tighter monetary policy expectations, increasing discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive despite sustained inflation acceleration above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly gross domestic product growth directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week as analyst earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of growth instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong monthly payroll growth and a rising unemployment rate indicates structural labor market friction, which historically precedes equity drawdowns over the next month as wage-push inflation emerges.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of instances showing this labor market divergence.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 500 thousand units signals a sharp aggregate demand shock, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as growth expectations collapse and industrial activity slows.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered instances despite the housing collapse.
